findings.title: CVE-2026-31431 Linux Kernel CopyFail — CISA Confirms Active Exploitation; Theori Publishes 100% Reliable Cross-Distro Exploit (Ubuntu 24.04 / Amazon Linux 2023 / RHEL 10.1 / SUSE 16)
findings.affected_technology: Linux kernel algif_aead (kernels 2017 → patch); Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL 10.1, SUSE 16, every modern cloud base image
findings.summary: CISA confirmed active in-the-wild exploitation of CopyFail and Theori published a 100%-reliable Python exploit working across Ubuntu 24.04, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL 10.1, and SUSE 16 ahead of the 2026-05-15 KEV deadline.

findings.title: Apache HTTP Server 9-CVE Patch Batch — HTTP/2 Double-Free RCE-Class (CVE-2026-23918) + 8 Others; Fixed in 2.4.67
findings.affected_technology: Apache HTTP Server (httpd) 2.4.0 through 2.4.66; mod_proxy_ajp, mod_auth_digest, HTTP/2, mod_rewrite/ap_expr, mod_authn_socache, mod_dav_lock
findings.summary: Apache shipped httpd 2.4.67 fixing nine CVEs led by CVE-2026-23918 (Important — HTTP/2 double-free with possible RCE on early reset) plus a mod_auth_digest timing-attack auth bypass and a .htaccess-author file-read primitive — patch within 7 days for internet-facing httpd.

findings.title: DigiCert Code-Signing Compromise — 60 Certificates Revoked, 11 Used to Sign Zhong Stealer Malware (Support-Portal Pivot via Customer-Chat Malware)
findings.affected_technology: DigiCert internal support portal; Code Signing certificate issuance; trust chain for any binaries signed by the 60 revoked certs
findings.summary: Attackers used a customer-chat malware payload to compromise DigiCert support analysts, pivoted to the internal portal, and fraudulently issued 60 Code Signing certificates — 11 of which were used to sign Zhong Stealer before revocation 2026-04-17.

findings.title: VENOMOUS#HELPER — Phishing-Delivered SimpleHelp / ScreenConnect IAB Campaign Targeting 80+ U.S. Organizations
findings.affected_technology: SimpleHelp Remote Support, ConnectWise ScreenConnect (legitimate RMM tools weaponised); user endpoints across U.S. enterprises
findings.summary: Securonix-tracked financially-motivated cluster VENOMOUS#HELPER is running an active Initial-Access-Broker campaign that uses phishing to trick users into installing customised SimpleHelp / ScreenConnect agents — bypassing EDR because the RMM binaries are legitimately signed and on most allowlists.

findings.title: CVE-2026-29200 Webpros Comet Backup — Cross-Tenant End-User Account Impersonation IDOR (CVSS 9.9)
findings.affected_technology: Webpros Comet Backup multi-tenant deployments; versions 20.11.0 → 26.1.0 / 26.2.0 (fixed 26.1.1 / 26.2.1)
findings.summary: A vulnerable API call in multi-tenant Comet Backup lets a tenant administrator impersonate any end-user account belonging to any other tenant on the same server (CVSS 4.0 = 9.9, no auth required from the attacker tenant's admin) — backups across hosting / MSP customers are the exposure.

findings.title: Trellix Discloses Source-Code Repository Breach — Latest in 2026 Security-Vendor Source-Code-Theft Cluster
findings.affected_technology: Trellix XDR / EDR product line (~50,000 customers / 200M+ endpoints)
findings.summary: Trellix disclosed unauthorized access to part of its source-code repository; the 2026 string of security-vendor source-code thefts (Checkmarx, Cisco, now Trellix) suggests EDR-bypass research will follow in weeks.

findings.title: Apache Thrift Triple Advisory — Rust DoS Pattern, Java SSL Hostname-Verification Bypass, Node.js web_server.js Multi-Vuln (CVE-2026-43868 / 43869 / 43870)
findings.affected_technology: Apache Thrift Rust, Java (TSSLTransportFactory), and Node.js (web_server.js) implementations; internal microservice / RPC meshes
findings.summary: Apache shipped three Thrift CVEs: the Rust port reproduces the 2020 DoS pattern, Java TSSLTransportFactory skips hostname verification (MITM enabler), and Node.js web_server.js has multiple unspecified flaws.

noted.product: cPanel & WHM (ongoing) — Now confirmed weaponized against Southeast Asian government, military, and MSP targets per The Hacker News; rfxn/cpanel-sessionscribe published as fleet-triage IOC scanner
noted.summary: Mass-exploitation continues with no score change vs. 2026-05-03 ransomware UPDATE; flagging because activity broadened to government/military/MSP victim profile.

Kaspersky reports a steep rise in attackers harvesting leaked AWS keys (TruffleHog scans of public GitHub / .env / Docker / S3) and sending DocuSign / BEC phishing through Amazon SES, bypassing reputation filters because all SES traffic shares legitimate AWS infrastructure.

noted.product: Microsoft Windows April 2026 updates breaking 3rd-party backup software (psmounterex.sys driver compatibility)
noted.summary: Operational fault rather than a vulnerability; affects Veeam / Acronis / similar drivers binding the psmounterex.sys driver structure — Microsoft confirmed and a fix is expected in the next 7-14 days.

noted.product: phpBB — Host Header Injection → Password-Reset Poisoning (CVSS 8.1)
noted.summary: Patched. Pre-auth via the password-reset flow; attacker poisons the Host header so reset-link email points to attacker-controlled URL → captures reset token → account takeover.

noted.product: Prefect — Missing Authentication on WebSocket Endpoint (CVSS 7.3)
noted.summary: Fixed in Prefect 3.6.14; DevOps workflow-orchestration platform's WebSocket endpoint exposed runs/logs without authentication — patch any self-hosted Prefect.

noted.product: FRRouting — BGP UPDATE Integer Underflow → Daemon DoS (CVSS 7.5)
noted.summary: Routing-disruption-class flaw; a peer in the BGP path can induce daemon crash via crafted UPDATE message — patch in next maintenance window.

noted.product: HashiCorp Boundary — TLS Handshake DoS Locks Worker Connections (CVSS 7.5)
noted.summary: Workers can be locked up via crafted TLS handshakes; impactful for any internet-reachable Boundary worker.

noted.product: Weaver E-cology — Unauthenticated RCE Active Since March (predominantly Chinese-deployed OA suite)
noted.summary: Vendor patched 2026-03-12; exploitation observed 2026-03-17 with whoami/ipconfig/tasklist recon and Goby-linked callbacks — limited Western footprint, included for completeness.

noted.product: Bluekit phishing kit (under development, AI-assistant + auto domain registration)
noted.summary: New phishing-as-a-service kit advertised with AI conversation steering and automated domain registration; not yet in active campaigns but represents the helpdesk-pretext attack class evolution.
Release announcement; specific CVE details not yet broken out — track for any MUA-side issue assigned a CVE in coming days.
